title: Kubernetes Pod Hostnames: Understanding Identity and DNS
summary: How Kubernetes assigns hostnames to Pods and how to use the hostname and subdomain fields to create predictable network identities.

title: Kubernetes Disruptions: Protecting Application Availability
summary: Understanding voluntary and involuntary disruptions and how to use Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) to ensure your app stays online during maintenance.

title: Kubernetes Ephemeral Containers: Debugging in Real-Time
summary: A guide to using Ephemeral Containers to debug running Pods without having to restart them or rebuild your images with debugging tools.

title: Kubernetes Sidecar Containers: Enhancing the Main App
summary: Understanding the sidecar pattern—how to use auxiliary containers to handle logging, monitoring, and security alongside your main application.

title: Kubernetes Init Containers: Preparing the Ground
summary: Learn how to use Init Containers to perform setup tasks, wait for dependencies, or populate volumes before your main app starts.

title: Kubernetes Pod Conditions: A Deeper Look at Health
summary: Going beyond phases—understanding the array of conditions that provide a detailed status report of a Pod's health.

title: Kubernetes Pod Lifecycle: From Pending to Terminated
summary: Understanding the various phases, conditions, and container states that define the life of a Kubernetes Pod.

title: Kubernetes Service Internal Traffic Policy: Optimizing Local Routing
summary: Exploring how the Internal Traffic Policy allows you to control whether Service traffic is routed cluster-wide or restricted to the local node.

title: Kubernetes ClusterIP Allocation: Preventing IP Collisions
summary: Understanding the internal logic of how Kubernetes allocates IP addresses to Services and the strategy used to avoid conflicts.

title: Kubernetes Networking on Windows: Bridging the OS Gap
summary: Understanding how Kubernetes handles networking for Windows containers, including the Host Networking Service (HNS) and differences from Linux.

title: Kubernetes Topology Aware Routing: Optimizing for Performance and Cost
summary: Learn how Topology Aware Routing helps reduce cross-zone traffic and latency by preferring endpoints in the same zone.

title: Kubernetes Dual-Stack: Bridging IPv4 and IPv6
summary: Exploring how Kubernetes supports both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses simultaneously for Pods and Services.

title: Kubernetes DNS: How Services and Pods Find Each Other
summary: A deep dive into the cluster's internal phonebook—understanding how DNS names are assigned to Services and Pods.

title: Kubernetes Network Policies: Securing the East-West Traffic
summary: Learn how to use Network Policies to control traffic flow between Pods and secure your cluster's internal communication.

title: Kubernetes EndpointSlices: Scaling Service Networking
summary: How EndpointSlices provide a more scalable and extensible way to track network endpoints in large Kubernetes clusters.

title: Kubernetes Gateway API: The Evolution of Service Networking
summary: An overview of the Kubernetes Gateway API—a role-oriented, expressive, and extensible evolution of traditional Ingress.

title: Understanding Kubernetes Ingress Controllers
summary: Exploring the engine behind Ingress resources—how Ingress Controllers actually implement your routing rules.

title: Kubernetes Ingress: Managing External Access
summary: An introduction to Kubernetes Ingress and how it manages external access to services in a cluster, typically HTTP.

title: Service in Kubernetes: Decoupling and Discovery
summary: Understanding how Kubernetes Services provide stable networking for volatile pods using labels and selectors.

title: Kubernetes 101: Control Plane and Worker Nodes
summary: A walkthrough of how the Kubernetes control plane coordinates scheduling and how worker nodes run your workloads.

title: Secure Kubernetes and Cloud Foundations
summary: A practical baseline for securing clusters and cloud primitives before you start layering on more advanced policy controls.

title: Kubernetes Networking: Services, Load Balancing, and Connectivity
summary: An overview of how Kubernetes handles networking, from the pod network model to services, ingress, and network policies.
